Meta.Title: 3-Day Trip Plan: Toronto
Meta.Summary: A balanced 3-day Toronto itinerary covering waterfront sights, core neighborhoods, museums, and flexible evening options with time for transit and meals.

Morning.Item: Start at St. Lawrence Market for breakfast and a walk through the historic district.
Walk to the Distillery Historic District for shops, architecture, and coffee.

Afternoon.Item: Head to the waterfront: Harbourfront Centre and a lakeside stroll.
Optional: Toronto Islands ferry for skyline views and a relaxed walk or bike ride.

Evening.Item: Dinner near King West or Queen West.
Optional: Sunset views from the CN Tower (book timed entry) or a waterfront patio in warm months.

Notes.Item: Use TTC or walking; keep CN Tower/Islands as weather-dependent options.

Morning.Item: Royal Ontario Museum (ROM) highlights and special exhibits.
Stroll through Yorkville for cafes and boutiques.

Afternoon.Item: Casa Loma visit for city views and gardens.
Walk part of the nearby ravine trails (weather permitting) for a nature break.

Evening.Item: Dinner along Bloor-Yorkville or in the Annex.
Optional: Catch a show (Mirvish) or live music depending on schedules.

Notes.Item: Prebook museum tickets on weekends; plan 30-45 minutes for TTC transfers.

Morning.Item: Kensington Market and Chinatown for brunch and street art.
Browse local shops and pick up snacks for later.

Afternoon.Item: Art Gallery of Ontario (AGO) or alternative: Graffiti Alley and Queen Street West shopping.
Relax in Trinity Bellwoods Park if weather is nice.

Evening.Item: Final dinner in Little Italy, Greektown (Danforth), or Ossington for a neighborhood send-off.
Optional: Night view from Polson Pier or a short waterfront walk to close the trip.

Notes.Item: Swap AGO with more neighborhood time if you prefer a lighter final day.
